place: Fishers Island CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Adriaen Block was the first recorded European visitor, who named it Vischer's Island in 1614 after one of his companions.
Hurricanes have played an important role in the island's history, with the Great September Gale of 1815 and the New England Hurricane of 1938 causing widespread damage.
The Ferguson Museum’s History Collection grew from donations after a 1960 appeal, now holding artifacts, photos, maps, and deeds that chronicle Fishers Island.
In 1870, a lifesaving station was erected at the island's western end by the State of Connecticut, overlooking waters between Fishers Island and Little Gull Island.
It lies at the eastern end of Long Island Sound, 2 miles off the southeastern coast of Connecticut, across Fishers Island Sound.
It is accessible from New London by plane and regular ferry service.
